title: Dragging down the full-screen blue title bar does not exit the full-screen mode.
author: HubKing
body_snippet:
If I use Microsoft's RDP client, in the full-screen mode, I can drag down the blue title bar at the top to exit the full-screen mode. mRemoteNG has a very similarly-looking blue title bar, but dragging down does not work.

## Expected Behavior
Dragging down the blue title bar exits the full-screen mode. MS's RDP client behaves that way, and in many of today's windowing system I have tried (Windows, Gnome, XFCE, etc), if a window is "maximised" and I drag down the title bar, the window becomes...

iterations.description: The custom full-screen toolbar does not implement dragging to exit full-screen mode, which deviates from standard Windows UI behavior for restoring maximized windows by dragging their title bar.

comments.author: PetbkA
comments.snippet:
**1.77.2** - yep, drag'n'dropping RDP header doesn't quit full screen. Ctrl + Alt + Break hotkey that exits full screen is working though, so the problem isn't critical.

As for implementation, it'll be a little complicated IMHO:
* In MS RDP client the client's _window_ is being drag'n'droped after quitting the full screen;
* In MRNG, due to it's mostly tab-oriented design, there could be the following behaviors:
    1. No RDP windows drag'n'drop will be done - drag'n'dropping RDP header wi...

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

notes:
AI triage (gemini): The custom full-screen toolbar does not implement dragging to exit full-screen mode, which deviates from standard Windows UI behavior for restoring maximized windows by dragging their title bar.
Approach: Implement custom drag logic (mouse down, mouse move, mouse up event handlers or WndPoc override) for the FullscreenToolbar to detect dragging, call FullscreenHandler.Value = false to exit full-screen, and reposition the main form accordingly.
